text:
The State Department is offering rewards up to $10 million for information on 10 top Iranian officials, including the new supreme leader, Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei. The offer, posted on the department’s website, does not specify what information would qualify, or whether it would have to lead to the capture or killing of the officials. It identifies the Iranians, including Ayatollah Khamenei and several people in his office, as leaders of the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps. Six are named, and four listed only by their positions.
In the weeks before U.S. and Israeli forces began their assault on Iran, President Trump had a message for Iranians protesting their government: “HELP IS ON ITS WAY.” Then, as he announced the opening strikes on Feb. 28, he called on the Iranian people to rise up.
“When we are finished, take over your government,” Mr. Trump said. “It will be yours to take.”
But on Friday, Mr. Trump appeared to acknowledge that his command was easier said than done.
title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
